original_hypothesis.title: Tax Rate Monotonicity Not Enforced Despite Documented Constraint
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setProjectTaxRates comment states rates are 'subject to only ever decreasing' but the code has no enforcement. Owner/factory can increase tax rates to 100% (10000 BP) at any time, effectively trapping holders who can only sell at total loss. This is a code bug: the documented invariant is not implemented.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Token holders rely on the stated property that tax rates can only decrease. The lack of enforcement allows rug-pull via tax rate manipulation. A launch at 2% tax attracting buyers, followed by increase to 99%, would devastate holders.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Comment says 'subject to only ever decreasing' but no check: newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectBuyTaxBasisPoints is missing

preconditions: Owner or factory calls setProjectTaxRates with higher values
Token has been launched with lower tax rates attracting holders

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: No comparison between old and new rates; direct assignment: projectBuyTaxBasisPoints = newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Launch with 1% tax, attract holders, then set tax to 99%
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Tax recipient captures 99% of all sell transactions

economic_questions: How many tokens have been launched with this code?
What is the typical token TVL at risk?
Is there any external enforcement mechanism?
validation_requirements: Confirm no require/assert checking old >= new rates
Test setting rates from 100 to 5000 BP
Verify increased tax applies immediately to next trade

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 99% of all sell volume captured as tax
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Very high on accumulated sell volume

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Token price collapse as holders cannot sell without massive tax

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All token holders attempting to sell
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 99% loss on sell transactions
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total market cap of the token

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Current tax rates across deployed tokens
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify no external contract enforces monotonicity

validation_result.mechanism: missing monotonicity enforcement on tax rates
validation_result.impact_summary: Owner or factory can increase tax rates arbitrarily despite documented constraint that rates should only decrease, enabling rug-pull via tax manipulation up to 100% (or beyond, causing underflow).
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The setProjectTaxRates function at AgentToken.sol lines 294-314 has a NatSpec comment stating 'Change the tax rates, subject to only ever decreasing' but the function body contains zero validation that the new rates are less than or equal to the old rates. The function simply assigns the new values directly: `projectBuyTaxBasisPoints = newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_` and `projectSellTaxBasisPoints = newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_`. There is no require, assert, or revert that enforces the documented monotonicity constraint. Additionally, there is no upper bound check ensuring the values stay below BP_DENOM (10000), meaning rates could theoretically be set above 100%, which would cause an underflow in the unchecked _taxProcessing block.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof-of-signal is accurate. Lines 294-314 of AgentToken.sol show the function with the comment 'subject to only ever decreasing' and no enforcement whatsoever. The function body at lines 305-313 simply stores old values, assigns new values, and emits an event. No comparison between old and new values exists anywhere in the function or any internal helper it calls.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Comment says 'subject to only ever decreasing' but no require/assert checks that newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectBuyTaxBasisPoints or newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectSellTaxBasisPoints. Rates can be freely increased.

The entire tax calculation is in an unchecked block. If projectSellTaxBasisPoints >= BP_DENOM (10000), then tax >= sentAmount_, and amountLessTax_ -= tax underflows to near uint256.max, causing massive token inflation to the receiver.

calling_sequence: 1. Owner or factory calls setProjectTaxRates(9900, 9900) - setting 99% tax
2. No validation occurs - old values are saved only for the event emission
3. projectBuyTaxBasisPoints and projectSellTaxBasisPoints are directly overwritten
4. On next sell: _transfer -> _taxProcessing computes tax = sentAmount * 9900 / 10000 = 99% of sentAmount
5. amountLessTax_ = sentAmount - tax = 1% of original - holder loses 99%
6. If rates set to 10001+: tax > sentAmount, amountLessTax_ underflows in unchecked block
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: setProjectTaxRates at line 303 accepts any uint16 values without comparison to existing rates. The onlyOwnerOrFactory modifier at line 65-69 only checks that the caller is the owner or factory - it does not enforce any rate constraints. After rates are changed, all subsequent transfers through _transfer (line 408) -> _taxProcessing (line 430) immediately apply the new rates. The unchecked block at line 437 means no overflow/underflow protection exists for the tax calculation.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deployment scripts (v2/deployFactory.ts, v2/deployMigrator.ts) set initial tax params via setTokenTaxParams using process.env.TAX values, but these are template params for new token creation only. No external mechanism enforces monotonicity on already-deployed AgentToken instances. The _tokenAdmin set during deployment is the owner of each deployed token and can call setProjectTaxRates freely.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack scenario is valid: 1) Token is deployed via factory with low initial tax (e.g., 2% = 200 BP). 2) Holders buy tokens attracted by low tax. 3) Owner/factory calls setProjectTaxRates(9900, 9900) setting 99% tax. 4) All sell transactions now lose 99% to tax. 5) Holders are effectively trapped. Even more extreme: setting rates to 10001+ causes underflow in the unchecked _taxProcessing block, potentially minting astronomical token amounts to the receiver while still extracting the full sentAmount as tax to the contract.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. This is a direct code defect where a documented invariant (monotonically decreasing tax rates) is not enforced. The impact is: (1) Owner can rug-pull by increasing sell tax to near-100%, trapping all holders. (2) Setting tax above 10000 BP causes arithmetic underflow in unchecked block, potentially breaking the entire token economy. (3) All deployed AgentToken instances are affected. (4) No timelock or governance delay protects against this - changes are immediate.
recommended_mitigations: Add monotonicity enforcement: require(newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectBuyTaxBasisPoints, 'Tax can only decrease'); require(newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_ <= projectSellTaxBasisPoints, 'Tax can only decrease');
Add upper bound validation: require(newProjectBuyTaxBasisPoints_ < BP_DENOM, 'Tax exceeds maximum'); require(newProjectSellTaxBasisPoints_ < BP_DENOM, 'Tax exceeds maximum');
Consider adding a timelock mechanism for any tax rate changes to give holders time to exit
Remove the unchecked block in _taxProcessing or add explicit bounds checking within it
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy AgentToken via factory with 2% buy/sell tax (200 BP). 2. Add initial liquidity. 3. Have user buy tokens on Uniswap pair. 4. As owner, call setProjectTaxRates(9900, 9900). 5. Have user attempt to sell - verify 99% is taken as tax. 6. Optionally test with 10001 BP to verify underflow behavior in unchecked block.
uncertainties: Whether the protocol team considers this an intentional design choice (owner trusted) vs. a bug - however, the NatSpec comment explicitly states the constraint should exist
Whether there is an off-chain monitoring system that could detect and respond to tax rate increases (this would not constitute a code-level fix)
additional_testing_needed: Test setting tax rates above 10000 BP and verify underflow behavior in _taxProcessing
Test interaction between increased tax rates and the autoswap mechanism
Verify that the factory address (set at initialization) cannot be changed, meaning only the original factory and owner can call this function
